{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_c99166868e0f4b2db28dc243","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Scope representation data structures\n2\t//!\n3\t//! This module defines the core data structures for representing hierarchical scopes\n4\t//! in YAML documents. These structures enable accurate duplicate key detection by\n5\t//! tracking keys within their proper scope contexts.\n6\t//!\n7\t//! # Overview\n8\t//!\n9\t//! YAML documents use indentation to define nested scopes. Keys in different scopes\n10\t//! can have the same name without being duplicates. For example:\n11\t//!\n12\t//! ```yaml\n13\t//! services:\n14\t//!   web:\n15\t//!     host: localhost    # \"host\" in services.web scope\n16\t//!     port: 8080\n17\t//!   database:\n18\t//!     host: db.example.com  # \"host\" in services.database scope (NOT a duplicate)\n19\t//!     port: 5432\n20\t//! ```\n21\t//!\n22\t//! The scope representation system tracks:\n23\t//! - **Scope**: A mapping context at a specific nesting level\n24\t//! - **ScopeStack**: A hierarchical stack of active scopes during parsing\n25\t//! - **KeyContext**: Classification of key types (inline scalar, parent mapping, parent sequence)\n26\t//!\n27\t//! # Architecture\n28\t//!\n29\t//! ## Scope\n30\t//!\n31\t//! A `Scope` represents a single mapping context with:\n32\t//! - Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n33\t//! - Set of keys defined within this scope\n34\t//! - Line number where this scope started\n35\t//! - Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n36\t//! - Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n37\t//!\n38\t//! ## ScopeStack\n39\t//!\n40\t//! A `ScopeStack` manages the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes:\n41\t//! - Maintains a stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n42\t//! - Tracks base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n43\t//! - Handles scope transitions (enter/exit) as indentation changes\n44\t//! - Provides duplicate key detection within proper scope contexts\n45\t//!\n46\t//! # Examples\n47\t//!\n48\t//! ```\n49\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{Scope, ScopeStack};\n50\t//!\n51\t//! let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // Base indent of 2 spaces\n52\t//!\n53\t//! // Enter a scope when encountering a parent mapping\n54\t//! stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n55\t//!\n56\t//! // Add keys to current scope\n57\t//! stack.add_key(\"web\", 2).unwrap();\n58\t//! stack.add_key(\"database\", 3).unwrap();\n59\t//!\n60\t//! // Detect duplicate in same scope\n61\t//! let result = stack.add_key(\"web\", 4);\n62\t//! assert!(result.is_err()); // Duplicate key error\n63\t//! ```\n64\t\n65\tuse std::collections::HashSet;\n66\tuse std::fmt;\n67\t\n68\t#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n69\tuse log::debug as log_debug;\n70\t#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n71\tuse log::warn as log_warn;\n72\t#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n73\tuse log::trace as log_trace;\n74\t\n75\t/// A scope representing a mapping context at a specific nesting level\n76\t///\n77\t/// Scopes are created when the parser encounters a parent mapping (a key whose value\n78\t/// is itself a mapping). Each scope maintains its own set of keys, independent of\n79\t/// keys in parent or sibling scopes.\n80\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n81\tpub struct Scope {\n82\t    /// Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n83\t    pub indent_level: usize,\n84\t    /// Keys defined within this scope\n85\t    pub keys: HashSet<String>,\n86\t    /// Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n87\t    pub start_line: usize,\n88\t    /// Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n89\t    pub parent_key: Option<String>,\n90\t    /// Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n91\t    pub is_flow_style: bool,\n92\t    /// Whether this scope is within a sequence context\n93\t    pub in_sequence_context: bool,\n94\t    /// Unique identifier for sequence items to distinguish them at same indent\n95\t    pub sequence_item_id: Option<usize>,\n96\t}\n97\t\n98\timpl Scope {\n99\t    /// Create a new scope\n100\t    ///\n101\t    /// # Arguments\n102\t    ///\n103\t    /// * `indent_level` - Number of leading spaces for this scope\n104\t    /// * `start_line` - Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n105\t    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n106\t    ///\n107\t    /// # Examples\n108\t    ///\n109\t    /// ```\n110\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n111\t    ///\n112\t    /// let scope = Scope::new(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n113\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.indent_level, 2);\n114\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.start_line, 5);\n115\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.parent_key, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n116\t    /// ```\n117\t    pub fn new(indent_level: usize, start_line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) -> Self {\n118\t        Self {\n119\t            indent_level,\n120\t            keys: HashSet::new(),\n121\t            start_line,\n122\t            parent_key,\n123\t            is_flow_style: false,\n124\t            in_sequence_context: false,\n125\t            sequence_item_id: None,\n126\t        }\n127\t    }\n128\t\n129\t    /// Add a key to this scope, returning true if it's a duplicate\n130\t    ///\n131\t    /// # Arguments\n132\t    ///\n133\t    /// * `key` - The key to add\n134\t    ///\n135\t    /// # Returns\n136\t    ///\n137\t    /// `true` if the key already exists in this scope (duplicate), `false` otherwise\n138\t    ///\n139\t    /// # Examples\n140\t    ///\n141\t    /// ```\n142\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n143\t    ///\n144\t    /// let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n145\t    ///\n146\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), false); // First occurrence\n147\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"second\"), false); // Different key\n148\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), true);  // Duplicate!\n149\t    /// ```\n150\t    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str) -> bool {\n151\t        !self.keys.insert(key.to_string())\n152\t    }\n153\t\n154\t    /// Check if this scope contains a key\n155\t    ///\n156\t    /// # Arguments\n157\t    ///\n158\t    /// * `key` - The key to check\n159\t    ///\n160\t    /// # Returns\n161\t    ///\n162\t    /// `true` if the key exists in this scope, `false` otherwise\n163\t    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n164\t        self.keys.contains(key)\n165\t    }\n166\t\n167\t    /// Get the number of keys in this scope\n168\t    pub fn key_count(&self) -> usize {\n169\t        self.keys.len()\n170\t    }\n171\t\n172\t    /// Clear all keys from this scope\n173\t    pub fn clear_keys(&mut self) {\n174\t        self.keys.clear();\n175\t    }\n176\t}\n177\t\n178\timpl fmt::Display for Scope {\n179\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n180\t        write!(f, \"Scope(indent={}, \", self.indent_level)?;\n181\t        if let Some(ref parent) = self.parent_key {\n182\t            write!(f, \"parent={}, \", parent)?;\n183\t        }\n184\t        write!(f, \"keys={})\", self.keys.len())\n185\t    }\n186\t}\n187\t\n188\t/// Hierarchical stack of active scopes\n189\t///\n190\t/// The ScopeStack maintains the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes during parsing.\n191\t/// As the parser encounters different indentation levels, it enters and exits scopes,\n192\t/// ensuring that duplicate key detection only considers keys within the same scope.\n193\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n194\tpub struct ScopeStack {\n195\t    /// Stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n196\t    scopes: Vec<Scope>,\n197\t    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n198\t    base_indent: usize,\n199\t    /// Sequence item counter for generating unique IDs\n200\t    sequence_item_counter: usize,\n201\t    /// History of indent transitions (whether or not they have keys)\n202\t    indent_transitions: Vec<IndentTransition>,\n203\t    /// The last recorded indent level (to detect transitions)\n204\t    last_indent: usize,\n205\t}\n206\t\n207\timpl ScopeStack {\n208\t    /// Create a new scope stack\n209\t    ///\n210\t    /// # Arguments\n211\t    ///\n212\t    /// * `base_indent` - The base indentation size in spaces (usually 2 or 4)\n213\t    ///\n214\t    /// # Examples\n215\t    ///\n216\t    /// ```\n217\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n218\t    ///\n219\t    /// let stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // 2-space indentation\n220\t    /// ```\n221\t    pub fn new(base_indent: usize) -> Self {\n222\t        Self {\n223\t            scopes: vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)], // Root scope\n224\t            base_indent,\n225\t            sequence_item_counter: 0,\n226\t            indent_transitions: Vec::new(),\n227\t            last_indent: 0,\n228\t        }\n229\t    }\n230\t\n231\t    /// Get the current scope (top of stack)\n232\t    ///\n233\t    /// # Panics\n234\t    ///\n235\t    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty (should never happen in normal operation)\n236\t    pub fn current_scope(&mut self) -> &mut Scope {\n237\t        self.scopes.last_mut().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n238\t    }\n239\t\n240\t    /// Get the current scope as an immutable reference\n241\t    ///\n242\t    /// # Panics\n243\t    ///\n244\t    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty\n245\t    pub fn current_scope_ref(&self) -> &Scope {\n246\t        self.scopes.last().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n247\t    }\n248\t\n249\t    /// Get scope for a specific indentation level\n250\t    ///\n251\t    /// # Arguments\n252\t    ///\n253\t    /// * `indent_level` - The indentation level to search for\n254\t    ///\n255\t    /// # Returns\n256\t    ///\n257\t    /// `Some(&Scope)` if a scope at this level exists, `None` otherwise\n258\t    pub fn get_scope_at_level(&self, indent_level: usize) -> Option<&Scope> {\n259\t        self.scopes.iter().find(|s| s.indent_level == indent_level)\n260\t    }\n261\t\n262\t    /// Enter a new scope (when indent increases)\n263\t    ///\n264\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters increased indentation,\n265\t    /// indicating entry into a nested scope.\n266\t    ///\n267\t    /// # Arguments\n268\t    ///\n269\t    /// * `indent_level` - The new indentation level\n270\t    /// * `line` - The line number where this scope starts (1-indexed)\n271\t    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n272\t    ///\n273\t    /// # Examples\n274\t    ///\n275\t    /// ```\n276\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n277\t    ///\n278\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n279\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n280\t    /// ```\n281\t    pub fn enter_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\n282\t        // Debug logging for scope entry\n283\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n284\t        {\n285\t            let parent_info = parent_key.as_ref().map(|k| k.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"<anonymous>\");\n286\t            let scope_type = \"Mapping\";\n287\t            log_debug!(\n288\t                \"[SCOPE ENTRY] type={}, line={}, indent={}, parent='{}', current_depth={}, path='{}'\",\n289\t                scope_type,\n290\t                line,\n291\t                indent_level,\n292\t                parent_info,\n293\t                self.depth(),\n294\t                self.get_scope_path()\n295\t            );\n296\t        }\n297\t\n298\t        // Check if we already have a scope at this level\n299\t        if let Some(existing) = self.get_scope_at_level(indent_level) {\n300\t            // We're re-entering a scope level - clear and reuse\n301\t            // This handles sibling mappings correctly\n302\t            let mut fresh_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n303\t            fresh_scope.is_flow_style = existing.is_flow_style;\n304\t\n305\t            // Remove all scopes deeper than this level\n306\t            let before_depth = self.depth();\n307\t            let before_path = self.get_scope_path();\n308\t\n309\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n310\t            {\n311\t                let scopes_to_remove: Vec<_> = self.scopes.iter()\n312\t                    .filter(|s| s.indent_level > indent_level)\n313\t                    .map(|s| format!(\"(indent={}, parent={:?})\", s.indent_level, s.parent_key))\n314\t                    .collect();\n315\t                if !scopes_to_remove.is_empty() {\n316\t                    log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Mapping reuse cleanup, removing {} scopes: {:?}\",\n317\t                             scopes_to_remove.len(),\n318\t                             scopes_to_remove);\n319\t                }\n320\t            }\n321\t\n322\t            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= indent_level);\n323\t\n324\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n325\t            {\n326\t                let after_retain_depth = self.depth();\n327\t                let removed_count = before_depth - after_retain_depth;\n328\t                if removed_count > 0 {\n329\t                    log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Mapping reuse cleanup, removed {} scopes: '{}' -> '{}'\",\n330\t                             removed_count,\n331\t                             before_path,\n332\t                             self.get_scope_path());\n333\t                }\n334\t            }\n335\t\n336\t            self.scopes.push(fresh_scope);\n337\t\n338\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n339\t            {\n340\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Mapping (reuse), indent={}, cleared scopes deeper than this level\", indent_level);\n341\t            }\n342\t        } else {\n343\t            // Create new scope\n344\t            let new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n345\t            self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n346\t\n347\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n348\t            {\n349\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Mapping (new), indent={}, created new scope at this level\", indent_level);\n350\t            }\n351\t        }\n352\t\n353\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n354\t        {\n355\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] After entry: depth={}, current_indent={}\", self.depth(), self.current_indent());\n356\t        }\n357\t    }\n358\t\n359\t    /// Exit to parent scope (when indent decreases)\n360\t    ///\n361\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters decreased indentation,\n362\t    /// indicating exit from a nested scope back to its parent.\n363\t    ///\n364\t    /// # Arguments\n365\t    ///\n366\t    /// * `target_indent` - The indentation level to exit to\n367\t    ///\n368\t    /// # Examples\n369\t    ///\n370\t    /// ```\n371\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n372\t    ///\n373\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n374\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n375\t    /// stack.exit_to_scope(0); // Exit back to root\n376\t    /// ```\n377\t    pub fn exit_to_scope(&mut self, target_indent: usize) {\n378\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n379\t        {\n380\t            log_debug!(\n381\t                \"[SCOPE EXIT] target_indent={}, current_depth={}, current_indent={}, path='{}'\",\n382\t                target_indent,\n383\t                self.depth(),\n384\t                self.current_indent(),\n385\t                self.get_scope_path()\n386\t            );\n387\t        }\n388\t\n389\t        // Edge case: can't exit to a deeper level than current\n390\t        if target_indent > self.current_indent() {\n391\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n392\t            {\n393\t                log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: target_indent={} > current_indent={}, ignoring exit request\", target_indent, self.current_indent());\n394\t            }\n395\t            return;\n396\t        }\n397\t\n398\t        // Edge case: can't exit if stack would become empty\n399\t        let would_be_empty = self.scopes.iter()\n400\t            .filter(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent)\n401\t            .count() == 0;\n402\t\n403\t        if would_be_empty {\n404\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n405\t            {\n406\t                log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: exit would empty scope stack, keeping at least root scope\");\n407\t            }\n408\t            // Keep at least the root scope\n409\t            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level == 0);\n410\t            return;\n411\t        }\n412\t\n413\t        // Remove all scopes deeper than target\n414\t        let before_depth = self.depth();\n415\t        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent);\n416\t\n417\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n418\t        {\n419\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] Removed {} scopes deeper than indent={}\", before_depth - self.depth(), target_indent);\n420\t        }\n421\t\n422\t        // Search for target scope in hierarchy\n423\t        // This handles cases where the exact target indent doesn't exist in the stack\n424\t        // by finding the closest parent scope or creating a fallback if needed\n425\t        if !self.scopes.iter().any(|s| s.indent_level == target_indent) {\n426\t            // No exact match at target indent - search for closest parent\n427\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n428\t            {\n429\t                log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: No scope found at target_indent={}, searching for closest parent scope\",\n430\t                         target_indent);\n431\t            }\n432\t\n433\t            // Find the closest scope with indent <= target_indent\n434\t            let closest_scope = self.scopes.iter()\n435\t                .filter(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent)\n436\t                .max_by_key(|s| s.indent_level);\n437\t\n438\t            match closest_scope {\n439\t                Some(scope) => {\n440\t                    // Found a parent scope - use that instead\n441\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n442\t                    {\n443\t                        log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] Found closest parent scope at indent={}, path='{}', using existing scope\",\n444\t                                 scope.indent_level, self.get_scope_path());\n445\t                    }\n446\t                    // We've already retained scopes <= target_indent, so we're at the closest scope\n447\t                }\n448\t                None => {\n449\t                    // No suitable parent scope found - this shouldn't happen in normal YAML\n450\t                    // but we handle it gracefully by creating a fallback scope\n451\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n452\t                    {\n453\t                        log_warn!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: No suitable parent scope found, creating fallback scope at indent={}\",\n454\t                                 target_indent);\n455\t                    }\n456\t                    let fallback_scope = Scope::new(target_indent, 0, None);\n457\t                    self.scopes.push(fallback_scope);\n458\t                }\n459\t            }\n460\t        }\n461\t\n462\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n463\t        {\n464\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] After exit: depth={}, current_indent={}, path='{}'\", self.depth(), self.current_indent(), self.get_scope_path());\n465\t        }\n466\t    }\n467\t\n468\t    /// Check if current scope contains a key\n469\t    ///\n470\t    /// # Arguments\n471\t    ///\n472\t    /// * `key` - The key to check for\n473\t    ///\n474\t    /// # Returns\n475\t    ///\n476\t    /// `true` if the key exists in the current scope, `false` otherwise\n477\t    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n478\t        self.scopes.last()\n479\t            .and_then(|scope| Some(scope.contains_key(key)))\n480\t            .unwrap_or(false)\n481\t    }\n482\t\n483\t    /// Check if any scope in the hierarchy contains a key\n484\t    ///\n485\t    /// This searches through all scopes in the stack, not just the current one.\n486\t    ///\n487\t    /// # Arguments\n488\t    ///\n489\t    /// * `key` - The key to search for\n490\t    ///\n491\t    /// # Returns\n492\t    ///\n493\t    /// `true` if the key exists in any scope, `false` otherwise\n494\t    pub fn contains_key_in_any_scope(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n495\t        self.scopes.iter().any(|scope| scope.contains_key(key))\n496\t    }\n497\t\n498\t    /// Add a key to current scope\n499\t    ///\n500\t    /// # Arguments\n501\t    ///\n502\t    /// * `key` - The key to add\n503\t    /// * `line` - The line number where the key appears (1-indexed)\n504\t    ///\n505\t    /// # Returns\n506\t    ///\n507\t    /// `Ok(())` if the key was added successfully\n508\t    /// `Err(DuplicateKeyError)` if the key already exists in the current scope\n509\t    ///\n510\t    /// # Examples\n511\t    ///\n512\t    /// ```\n513\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n514\t    ///\n515\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n516\t    ///\n517\t    /// // First occurrence succeeds\n518\t    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 1).is_ok());\n519\t    ///\n520\t    /// // Second occurrence fails\n521\t    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\"host\", 2).is_err());\n522\t    /// ```\n523\t    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str, line: usize) -> Result<(), DuplicateKeyError> {\n524\t        let scope_path = self.get_scope_path();\n525\t\n526\t        // Check if key exists before adding\n527\t        if self.contains_key(key) {\n528\t            let scope = self.current_scope();\n529\t            Err(DuplicateKeyError {\n530\t                key: key.to_string(),\n531\t                scope_path,\n532\t                first_line: scope.start_line,\n533\t                duplicate_line: line,\n534\t            })\n535\t        } else {\n536\t            let scope = self.current_scope();\n537\t            scope.add_key(key);\n538\t            Ok(())\n539\t        }\n540\t    }\n541\t\n542\t    /// Get human-readable path to current scope\n543\t    ///\n544\t    /// Returns a dot-separated path representing the scope hierarchy,\n545\t    /// e.g., \"services.web.database\" for a deeply nested scope.\n546\t    ///\n547\t    /// # Returns\n548\t    ///\n549\t    /// A string representing the scope path\n550\t    ///\n551\t    /// # Examples\n552\t    ///\n553\t    /// ```\n554\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n555\t    ///\n556\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n557\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n558\t    /// stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\"web\".to_string()));\n559\t    /// assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \"services.web\");\n560\t    /// ```\n561\t    pub fn get_scope_path(&self) -> String {\n562\t        let mut path = Vec::new();\n563\t        for scope in &self.scopes {\n564\t            if let Some(ref key) = scope.parent_key {\n565\t                path.push(key.clone());\n566\t            }\n567\t        }\n568\t        path.join(\".\")\n569\t    }\n570\t\n571\t    /// Get current indent level\n572\t    ///\n573\t    /// # Returns\n574\t    ///\n575\t    /// The indentation level of the current scope\n576\t    pub fn current_indent(&self) -> usize {\n577\t        self.scopes.last()\n578\t            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level)\n579\t            .unwrap_or(0)\n580\t    }\n581\t\n582\t    /// Get the number of active scopes in the stack\n583\t    pub fn depth(&self) -> usize {\n584\t        self.scopes.len()\n585\t    }\n586\t\n587\t    /// Clear all scopes and reset to root\n588\t    ///\n589\t    /// This resets the scope stack to its initial state with only the root scope.\n590\t    pub fn reset(&mut self) {\n591\t        self.scopes = vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)];\n592\t        self.clear_indent_transitions();\n593\t    }\n594\t\n595\t    /// Get the base indentation size\n596\t    pub fn base_indent(&self) -> usize {\n597\t        self.base_indent\n598\t    }\n599\t\n600\t    /// Set the base indentation size\n601\t    ///\n602\t    /// # Arguments\n603\t    ///\n604\t    /// * `base_indent` - The new base indentation size in spaces\n605\t    pub fn set_base_indent(&mut self, base_indent: usize) {\n606\t        self.base_indent = base_indent;\n607\t    }\n608\t\n609\t    /// Enter a sequence context (when we see a `-` item)\n610\t    ///\n611\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters a sequence item,\n612\t    /// creating a new scope for that item to prevent false duplicate detection\n613\t    /// between items at the same indentation level.\n614\t    ///\n615\t    /// # Arguments\n616\t    ///\n617\t    /// * `indent_level` - The indentation level of the sequence item\n618\t    /// * `line` - The line number where this sequence item starts (1-indexed)\n619\t    ///\n620\t    /// # Examples\n621\t    ///\n622\t    /// ```\n623\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n624\t    ///\n625\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n626\t    /// stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5); // Enter sequence item at indent 2\n627\t    /// ```\n628\t    pub fn enter_sequence_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize) {\n629\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n630\t        {\n631\t            log_debug!(\n632\t                \"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, line={}, indent={}, current_depth={}, path='{}'\",\n633\t                line,\n634\t                indent_level,\n635\t                self.depth(),\n636\t                self.get_scope_path()\n637\t            );\n638\t        }\n639\t\n640\t        // Remove all scopes at or deeper than this level\n641\t        // When entering a sequence scope, we clear any existing scope at this level\n642\t        // (including parent mappings) and all deeper scopes, then add a new sequence scope\n643\t        let before_depth = self.depth();\n644\t        let before_path = self.get_scope_path();\n645\t\n646\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n647\t        {\n648\t            let scopes_to_remove: Vec<_> = self.scopes.iter()\n649\t                .filter(|s| s.indent_level >= indent_level)\n650\t                .map(|s| format!(\"(indent={}, parent={:?})\", s.indent_level, s.parent_key))\n651\t                .collect();\n652\t            if !scopes_to_remove.is_empty() {\n653\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Sequence entry cleanup, removing {} scopes at or deeper than indent={}: {:?}\",\n654\t                         scopes_to_remove.len(),\n655\t                         indent_level,\n656\t                         scopes_to_remove);\n657\t            }\n658\t        }\n659\t\n660\t        // Only retain scopes shallower than indent_level\n661\t        // Sequence scopes completely replace any mapping at the same level\n662\t        // (unlike sibling mappings which preserve the parent)\n663\t        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level < indent_level);\n664\t\n665\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n666\t        {\n667\t            let removed_count = before_depth - self.depth();\n668\t            if removed_count > 0 {\n669\t                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Sequence entry cleanup, removed {} scopes: '{}' -> '{}'\",\n670\t                         removed_count,\n671\t                         before_path,\n672\t                         self.get_scope_path());\n673\t            }\n674\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, indent={}, cleared scopes at or deeper than this level (replaces any parent mapping)\", indent_level);\n675\t        }\n676\t\n677\t        // Always create a new scope for each sequence item\n678\t        // Each sequence item needs a fresh scope - we don't reuse existing sequence scopes\n679\t        let mut new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, None);\n680\t        new_scope.in_sequence_context = true;\n681\t        self.sequence_item_counter += 1;\n682\t        new_scope.sequence_item_id = Some(self.sequence_item_counter);\n683\t        self.scopes.push(new_scope);\n684\t\n685\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n686\t        {\n687\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence (new), item_id={}, created new sequence scope\", self.sequence_item_counter);\n688\t        }\n689\t\n690\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n691\t        {\n692\t            log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, after entry: depth={}, in_sequence={}\", self.depth(), self.in_sequence_context());\n693\t        }\n694\t    }\n695\t\n696\t    /// Check if we're in a sequence context\n697\t    ///\n698\t    /// # Returns\n699\t    ///\n700\t    /// `true` if the current scope is within a sequence context, `false` otherwise\n701\t    pub fn in_sequence_context(&self) -> bool {\n702\t        self.scopes.last()\n703\t            .map(|scope| scope.in_sequence_context)\n704\t            .unwrap_or(false)\n705\t    }\n706\t\n707\t    /// Record an indent transition\n708\t    ///\n709\t    /// This method tracks indentation level changes during parsing, whether or not\n710\t    /// they occur on lines with key tokens. This enables detection of indent changes\n711\t    /// on blank lines, comments, or other non-key lines.\n712\t    ///\n713\t    /// # Arguments\n714\t    ///\n715\t    /// * `line_number` - The line number where this transition occurred (1-indexed)\n716\t    /// * `new_indent` - The new indentation level\n717\t    /// * `has_key` - Whether this transition occurred on a line with a key token\n718\t    /// * `raw_line` - The raw line content (for debugging)\n719\t    ///\n720\t    /// # Examples\n721\t    ///\n722\t    /// ```\n723\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n724\t    ///\n725\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n726\t    /// stack.record_indent_transition(5, 2, false, \"  # blank line with indent\");\n727\t    /// ```\n728\t    pub fn record_indent_transition(&mut self, line_number: usize, new_indent: usize, has_key: bool, raw_line: &str) {\n729\t        // Only record if the indent actually changed\n730\t        if new_indent != self.last_indent {\n731\t            let transition = IndentTransition::new(\n732\t                line_number,\n733\t                self.last_indent,\n734\t                new_indent,\n735\t                has_key,\n736\t                raw_line,\n737\t            );\n738\t            self.indent_transitions.push(transition);\n739\t            self.last_indent = new_indent;\n740\t\n741\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n742\t            {\n743\t                let direction = if new_indent > self.last_indent.saturating_sub(1) {\n744\t                    \"increase\"\n745\t                } else {\n746\t                    \"decrease\"\n747\t                };\n748\t                let key_status = if has_key { \"with-key\" } else { \"without-key\" };\n749\t                log_debug!(\n750\t                    \"[INDENT TRANSITION] line={}, {}→{}, {}, raw='{}'\",\n751\t                    line_number,\n752\t                    self.last_indent.saturating_sub(1),\n753\t                    new_indent,\n754\t                    key_status,\n755\t                    raw_line.trim()\n756\t                );\n757\t            }\n758\t        }\n759\t    }\n760\t\n761\t    /// Get all recorded indent transitions\n762\t    ///\n763\t    /// # Returns\n764\t    ///\n765\t    /// A slice of all indent transitions recorded during parsing\n766\t    pub fn get_indent_transitions(&self) -> &[IndentTransition] {\n767\t        &self.indent_transitions\n768\t    }\n769\t\n770\t    /// Get indent transitions without keys\n771\t    ///\n772\t    /// Returns only those transitions that occurred on lines without key tokens,\n773\t    /// such as blank lines or comments.\n774\t    ///\n775\t    /// # Returns\n776\t    ///\n777\t    /// A vector of indent transitions that occurred without keys\n778\t    pub fn get_transitions_without_keys(&self) -> Vec<&IndentTransition> {\n779\t        self.indent_transitions\n780\t            .iter()\n781\t            .filter(|t| t.is_without_key())\n782\t            .collect()\n783\t    }\n784\t\n785\t    /// Get indent transitions with keys\n786\t    ///\n787\t    /// Returns only those transitions that occurred on lines with key tokens.\n788\t    ///\n789\t    /// # Returns\n790\t    ///\n791\t    /// A vector of indent transitions that occurred with keys\n792\t    pub fn get_transitions_with_keys(&self) -> Vec<&IndentTransition> {\n793\t        self.indent_transitions\n794\t            .iter()\n795\t            .filter(|t| t.has_key)\n796\t            .collect()\n797\t    }\n798\t\n799\t    /// Clear all indent transitions\n800\t    ///\n801\t    /// This is typically called when resetting the scope stack for a new document.\n802\t    pub fn clear_indent_transitions(&mut self) {\n803\t        self.indent_transitions.clear();\n804\t        self.last_indent = 0;\n805\t    }\n806\t\n807\t    /// Get the last recorded indent level\n808\t    ///\n809\t    /// # Returns\n810\t    ///\n811\t    /// The most recent indent level recorded\n812\t    pub fn get_last_indent(&self) -> usize {\n813\t        self.last_indent\n814\t    }\n815\t\n816\t    /// Set the last indent level directly\n817\t    ///\n818\t    /// This can be used to initialize the indent tracking or to correct it after\n819\t    /// document markers or other special constructs.\n820\t    ///\n821\t    /// # Arguments\n822\t    ///\n823\t    /// * `indent` - The indent level to set\n824\t    pub fn set_last_indent(&mut self, indent: usize) {\n825\t        self.last_indent = indent;\n826\t    }\n827\t\n828\t    /// Process an indent transition without a key token\n829\t    ///\n830\t    /// This method handles indentation changes that occur on lines without key tokens,\n831\t    /// such as blank lines or comments. It determines if the indent change represents\n832\t    /// a valid scope transition and triggers the appropriate scope entry/exit.\n833\t    ///\n834\t    /// # Arguments\n835\t    ///\n836\t    /// * `line_number` - The line number where this transition occurred (1-indexed)\n837\t    /// * `new_indent` - The new indentation level\n838\t    ///\n839\t    /// # Returns\n840\t    ///\n841\t    /// `true` if a scope transition occurred, `false` otherwise\n842\t    ///\n843\t    /// # Examples\n844\t    ///\n845\t    /// ```\n846\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n847\t    ///\n848\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n849\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"parent\".to_string()));\n850\t    /// // Process indent decrease on blank line\n851\t    /// let transitioned = stack.process_indent_transition_without_key(5, 0);\n852\t    /// assert!(transitioned); // Should exit to root scope\n853\t    /// ```\n854\t    pub fn process_indent_transition_without_key(&mut self, line_number: usize, new_indent: usize) -> bool {\n855\t        use std::cmp::Ordering;\n856\t\n857\t        // Compare new indent with current scope indent\n858\t        let current_indent = self.current_indent();\n859\t\n860\t        match new_indent.cmp(&current_indent) {\n861\t            Ordering::Greater => {\n862\t                // Indent increased - but without a key, this is unusual\n863\t                // We don't enter a new scope without a parent key\n864\t                // Just record the transition for tracking purposes\n865\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n866\t                {\n867\t                    log_debug!(\n868\t                        \"[INDENT WITHOUT KEY] line={}, indent={}→{} (increase), NO SCOPE ENTRY (no parent key)\",\n869\t                        line_number, current_indent, new_indent\n870\t                    );\n871\t                }\n872\t                false\n873\t            }\n874\t            Ordering::Less => {\n875\t                // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n876\t                // This is valid even without a key (e.g., blank line at end of nested block)\n877\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n878\t                {\n879\t                    let before_path = self.get_scope_path();\n880\t                    log_debug!(\n881\t                        \"[INDENT WITHOUT KEY] line={}, indent={}→{} (decrease), exiting scope: '{}'\",\n882\t                        line_number, current_indent, new_indent, before_path\n883\t                    );\n884\t                }\n885\t\n886\t                self.exit_to_scope(new_indent);\n887\t                true\n888\t            }\n889\t            Ordering::Equal => {\n890\t                // Same indent - no scope change needed\n891\t                false\n892\t            }\n893\t        }\n894\t    }\n895\t}\n896\t\n897\timpl fmt::Display for ScopeStack {\n898\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n899\t        write!(f, \"ScopeStack(depth={}, base_indent={}, current_path={})\",\n900\t               self.depth(), self.base_indent, self.get_scope_path())\n901\t    }\n902\t}\n903\t\n904\t/// Error when a duplicate key is detected\n905\t///\n906\t/// This error is returned when attempting to add a key that already exists\n907\t/// in the current scope.\n908\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n909\tpub struct DuplicateKeyError {\n910\t    /// The duplicate key\n911\t    pub key: String,\n912\t    /// The scope path where the duplicate was found\n913\t    pub scope_path: String,\n914\t    /// The line number where the key was first defined\n915\t    pub first_line: usize,\n916\t    /// The line number where the duplicate was found\n917\t    pub duplicate_line: usize,\n918\t}\n919\t\n920\timpl DuplicateKeyError {\n921\t    /// Create a new duplicate key error\n922\t    ///\n923\t    /// # Arguments\n924\t    ///\n925\t    /// * `key` - The duplicate key name\n926\t    /// * `scope_path` - The scope path where the duplicate was found\n927\t    /// * `first_line` - Line number of first occurrence\n928\t    /// * `duplicate_line` - Line number of duplicate occurrence\n929\t    pub fn new(key: String, scope_path: String, first_line: usize, duplicate_line: usize) -> Self {\n930\t        Self {\n931\t            key,\n932\t            scope_path,\n933\t            first_line,\n934\t            duplicate_line,\n935\t        }\n936\t    }\n937\t\n938\t    /// Get a formatted error message\n939\t    ///\n940\t    /// # Returns\n941\t    ///\n942\t    /// A human-readable error message\n943\t    pub fn message(&self) -> String {\n944\t        format!(\n945\t            \"Line {}: duplicate key '{}' in scope DuplicateKeyError {}\n962\t\n963\t/// Key context classification\n964\t///\n965\t/// Enumerates the different contexts in which a key can appear in a YAML document.\n966\t/// This classification helps the parser understand how to handle different key types.\n967\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]\n968\tpub enum KeyContext {\n969\t    /// Key with inline scalar value: \"key: value\"\n970\t    ///\n971\t    /// The key has a simple scalar value on the same line\n972\t    InlineScalar {\n973\t        /// The key name\n974\t        key: String,\n975\t        /// The scalar value\n976\t this key)\n982\t    ParentMapping {\n983\t        /// The parent key name\n984\t        key: String,\n985\t    },\n986\t\n987\t    /// Key with nested sequence: \"key:\\n  - item1\"\n988\t    ///\n989\t    /// The key's value is a sequence of items\n990\t    ParentSequence {\n991\t        /// The parent key name\n992\t        key: String,\n993\t    },\n994\t}\n995\t\n996\timpl KeyContext {\n997\t    /// Get the key name from any key context variant\n998\t    ///\n999\t    /// # Returns\n1000\t    ///\n1001\t    /// The key name as a string slice\n1002\t    pub fn key_name(&self) -> &str {\n1003\t        match self {\n1004\t            KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, .. } => key,\n1005\t            KeyContext::ParentMapping { key } => key,\n1006\t            KeyContext::ParentSequence { key } => key,\n1007\t        }\n1008\t    }\n1009\t\n1010\t    /// Check if this is a parent key (creates a new scope)\n1011\t    ///\n1012\t    /// # Returns\n1013\t    ///\n1014\t    /// `true` if this key creates a new scope (mapping or sequence)\n1015\t    pub fn is_parent_key(&self) -> bool {\n1016\t        matches!(self, KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. } | KeyContext::ParentSequence { .. })\n1017\t    }\n1018\t\n1019\t    /// Check if this is an inline scalar (does not create a new scope)\n1020\t    ///\n1021\t    /// # Returns\n1022\t    ///\n1023\t    /// `true` if this key has an inline scalar value\n1024\t    pub fn is_inline_scalar(&self) -> bool {\n1025\t        matches!(self, KeyContext::InlineScalar { .. })\n1026\t    }\n1027\t}\n1028\t\n1029\timpl fmt::Display for KeyContext {\n1030\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n1031\t        match self {\n1032\t position\n1075\t    let colon_pos = trimmed.find(':')?;\n1076\t    let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n1077\t    let after_colon = &trimmed[colon_pos + 1..];\n1078\t\n1079\t    // Skip if key is empty\n1080\t    let key = key_part.trim();\n1081\t    if key.is_empty() {\n1082\t        return None;\n1083\t    }\n1084\t\n1085\t    // Skip if key contains invalid characters (like in flow style)\n1086\t    if key.contains('{') || key.contains('}') || key.contains('[') || key.contains(']') {\n1087\t        return None;\n1088\t    }\n1089\t\n1090\t    // Strip sequence dash from key if present\n1091\t    // Handles: \"- name: value\" -> key should be \"name\", not \"- name\"\n1092\t    let actual_key = if key.starts_with(\"- \") {\n1093\t        // Remove the \"- \" prefix (dash and space)\n1094\t        key[2..].trim()\n1095\t    } else if key.starts_with('-') && key.len() > 1 {\n1096\t        // Dash followed by non-space (invalid, but handle gracefully)\n1097\t        &key[1..]\n1098\t    } else {\n1099\t        key\n1100\t    };\n1101\t\n1102\t    // Skip if actual key is empty after stripping dash\n1103\t    if actual_key.is_empty() {\n1104\t        return        }\n1115\t    };\n1116\t\n1117\t    Some(context)\n1118\t}\n1119\t\n1120\t/// Get leading whitespace length from a line\n1121\t///\n1122\t/// # Arguments\n1123\t///\n1124\t/// * `line` - The Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n1148\tpub struct IndentTransition {\n1149\t    /// The line number where this transition occurred (1-indexed)\n1150\t    pub line_number: usize,\n1151\t    /// The previous `from_indent` - The previous indentation level\n1168\t    /// * `to_indent` - The new indentation level\n1169\t    /// * `has_key` - Whether this transition occurred on a line with a key token\n1170\t    /// * `raw_line` - The raw line content\n1171\t    pub fn new(line_number: usize, from_indent: usize, to_indent: usize, has_key: bool, raw_line: &str) -> Self {\n1172\t        Self {\n1173\t            line_number,\n1174\t            from_indent,\n1175\t            to_indent,\n1176\t            has_key,\n1177\t            raw_line: raw_line.to_string(),\n1178\t        }\n1179\t    }\n1180\t\n1181\t    /// Check if this is an indent increase\n1182\t    pub fn is_increase(&self) -> bool {\n1183\t        self.to_indent > self.from_indent\n1184\t    }\n1185\t\n1186\t    /// Check if this is an indent decrease\n1187\t    pub fn is_decrease(&self) -> bool {\n1188\t        self.to_indent < self.from_indent\n1189\t    }\n1190\t\n1191\t    /// Get the indent change amount (positive for increase, negative for decrease)\n1192\t    pub fn change_amount(&self) -> isize {\n1193\t        self.to_indent as isize - self.from_indent as isize\n1194\t    }\n1195\t\n1196\t    /// Check if this transition occurred without a key\n1197\t    pub fn is_without_key(&self) -> bool {\n1198\t        !self.has_key\n1199\t    }\n1200\t}\n1201\t\n1202\timpl fmt::Display for IndentTransition {\n1203\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n1204\t        let direction = if self.is_increase() {\n1205\t            \"increase\"\n1206\t        } else if self.is_decrease() {\n1207\t            \"decrease\"\n1208\t        } else {\n1209\t            \"no-change\"\n1210\t        };\n1211\t        let key_status = if self.has_key { \"with-key\" } else {
